statement.markdown: Implement a function that takes a string as input and returns the reversed string. The function should handle strings of any length and should not use any built-in string reversal methods.
statement.notes: The function should handle null and empty strings correctly.

examples.explanation: The function should reverse the input string
The function should handle empty strings correctly
The function should handle null strings correctly

hints.text: Use a loop to iterate over the input string

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to use a loop to iterate over the input string and build the reversed string from the end.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute-force solution is to use a loop to iterate over the input string and append each character to the result string.
editorial.betterSolution: We can improve the solution by using a two-pointer approach, one starting from the beginning of the string and one from the end.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a two-pointer approach with a single loop, iterating over the input string and building the reversed string.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The solution is correct because it handles all edge cases correctly and reverses the input string correctly.
editorial.dryRun: Let's try the solution with the example input 'Hello World' to see how it works.
commonMistakes: Not handling null and empty strings correctly
